criteria_checked.note: Carried ruling: the overnight envelope names this docs cycle verbatim in the ratified menu; approver recorded in the plan; dedicated branch; push/CI/FF reserved to morning human gates. Replica/live 802/3/3 still mandated even for a docs cycle per the envelope — stricter than the docs-only --all-SKIP precedent, which is lawful (stricter never needs a hatch).

Ruling on the SHIPPED doc: the planned reference shapes are LAWFUL. The manifest discipline requires host-operating .md dependencies to resolve to bundled files; the existing :72-74 shape (a pointer to the unshipped disclosure ledger a host never navigates to) is the blessed breadcrumb precedent, and the plan mandates mirroring it. VERIFIED MACHINE FACT: the m8 dangling scan reads adapters, .agents/skills, .claude/skills, .claude/agents, DMC.md, CLAUDE.md — it never scans the installed docs/ content, so the m8-suite AC lane cannot machine-verify the new section's outbound references; the plan's Assumption discloses exactly this and names the compensating check (critic/verifier reading + the breadcrumb-shape AC). Compensating check REQUIRED at build sign-off: extract every path-like .md reference from the ADDED lines and prove each bundled-or-breadcrumb.

Ruling on where the honest line sits: LAWFUL are dated, build-pinned, session-scoped, past-tense observation claims (dispatch observed five-of-five and both envelope classes honored IN THAT consented 2026-07-09 session at cli 0.132.0), matching the MILESTONES record verbatim; official-docs mechanism facts (layer merge, content-hash trust) are separately citable as standing facts. UNLAWFUL (promotion-by-wording) would be present-tense standing behavior claims, cross-build generalization, or any wording implying the shims became a runtime boundary. MANDATORY qualifiers wherever dispatch/honoring is described: the date, the build pins, one-consented-session scoping, and the posture line restated (adapter remains ADVISORY, pre-commit/CI the boundary, promotion a registered future gate). The plan mandates all of these; the D5 no-edit row on ENFORCEMENT_MATRIX/doctor/shims is the structural backstop.

Ruling: the dated closure sub-note UNDER item-10(e) plus the new register entries is the LAWFUL append form, and it is BETTER than a new-entry-only form, which would leave a stale UNPROVEN claim standing without a pointer. The ledger discipline is append-never-drop: the original bullet text must stay byte-intact, the sub-note is purely additive line(s), no renumbering or reflow of existing sub-bullets, and the diff must show zero deletions in the ledger block — exactly the shape the plan's AC asserts.

Verified: no tool asserts on the CONTENT of the three edited docs — every in-repo reference is a comment or pointer; the v0.4.8 legacy interop-doc check greps docs/INTEROP.md only (untouched), so the pinned legacy 802/3/3 cannot move on doc edits; linkcheck's 24-file set excludes all three; the constitution's modes-table pin (OMC_COEXISTENCE.md:10-16) survives because the new section APPENDS at file end.

Docs-only, 4 editable files, single owner per file across the three tasks (MILESTONES touched only by T003); no G4 override needed (docs paths absent from DEFAULT_PROTECTED; MILESTONES landmark FLAG expected and non-degrading); facts to record verified against the Option-B MILESTONES entry — the plan's fact list matches the record, including the App zero-dispatch corroboration and the Ring-2 respected line.
